Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-21-2010 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-29-2010 Continued To Dec-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ingrid Eberle from the Law Ofices of Roger Meredith and associated Counsel for Farley Tolpen for and with Phyllis Hampton.

Ms. Eberle stated that they were still requesting to be relieved as Counsel, that there was not much left outstanding, that they had a difficult time communicating with Ms. Hampton, that she believed Ms. Hampton filed the taxes but that she had seen no evidence of filing of the tax return, and that filing of the past taxes would take a long time to prepare. In addition, Ms. Eberle stated that the Franchise Tax Board will not waive their claim until the return is processed, that she does not believe that the escheated account was part of the Estate and that it appears to be held in Trust. Ms. Hampton stated that she has provided everything that the Attorney has asked for and that she paid the taxes. The Court directed Ms. Hampton to provide copies of the documents regarding title of the property, stated that it would not grant the request to be relieved as Counsel at this time and that Ms. Hampton can waive an accounting as she is the sole Beneficiary so that they can proceed to close this estate. The matter was continued to December 21, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the Franchise Tax Board claim and for filing of the Petition for Final Distribution.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Jun-29-2010 Continued To Dec-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-13-2010 Continued To Jun-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Meredith for Phyllis Hampton.

Mr. Meredith stated that he was associated in as Counsel, that he had not substituted in and that he was not asking the Court to rule on the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel. In addition, he stated that Ms. Hampton told him that the state tax return had been filed, that the Bank would not allow Her to access the CD account and that the CD account was escheated to the state. Mr. Meredith also stated that he was not sure if the CD account was a pay-on-death account or part of the Estate, that the accounting would only be for the house and the settlement, and that there was an issue with title on the property. The Court encouraged Ms. Hampton to cooperate with Mr. Meredith. The matters were continued to June 15, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-20-2009 Continued To Nov-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ingrdi Eberle for Phyllis Hampton (Petitioner).

Ms. Eberle requested a continuance stating that they had been associated in as Counsel in August, that she recently received all of the files, that she has reviewed and has made attempts to contact her Client, but that her Client had not responded. The matter was continued to November 17, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed Counsel to amend the petition to reflect her representation and stated that it needed to be signed by her Client.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

For Attorney'S Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Farley S. Tolpen And Phyllis Hampton In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-30-2006 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Farley Tolpen appearing for Phyllis Hampton. Frances An appearing for and with Brenda Cannon. Mr. Cannon wants to speak for his wife. Court will not allow Mr. Cannon to speak since he is not a party. Brenda Cannon has refused to sign another deed. Kerry Cannon is deceased. On enforcement of the settlement, deed was signed. The parties has complied with the terms. It was a wrong deed prepared by Mr. Tolpen's office. Court is denying the motion to enforce settlement. Compliance was met. Sanctions denied. Ms. An to prepare an order. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
